chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Legal Punishment for Slanderous Accusation

text: 5068 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ صَفْوَانَ عَنْ أَبِي بَكْرٍ اَلْحَضْرَمِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ يَفْتَرِي عَلَى رَجُلٍ مِنْ جَاهِلِيَّةِ اَلْعَرَبِ قَالَ "يُضْرَبُ حَدّاً" قُلْتُ يُضْرَبُ حَدّاً قَالَ "نَعَمْ إِنَّ ذَلِكَ يَدْخُلُ عَلَى رَسُولِ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5068 - It was narrated from Safwan from Abu Bakr Al-Hadrami from Abu Abdullah (as) who said: I asked Imam (as) about a man who slanders a man from the pre-Islamic Arabs.
Imam (as) said: "He is to be given the legal punishment."
I asked: "Is he to be given the legal punishment?"
He said: "Yes, because that offense reaches the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family)."
